segments.text: TUTOR: OK, Jim. You wanted to see me about your textile design project.
segments.translation: 导师：好，吉姆。你想和我谈谈你的纺织品设计项目。

segments.text: JIM: That's right.
segments.translation: 吉姆：是的。

segments.text: I've been looking at how a range of natural dyes can be used to colour fabrics like cotton and wool.
segments.translation: 我一直在研究如何用各种天然染料给棉花和羊毛等织物上色。

segments.text: TUTOR: Why did you choose that topic?
segments.translation: 导师：你为什么选择这个话题?

segments.text: JIM: Well, I got a lot of useful ideas from the museum, you know, at that exhibition of textiles.
segments.translation: 吉姆：我从博物馆得到了很多有用的想法，你知道的，就是那个纺织品展览。

segments.text: But I've always been interested in anything to do with colour.
segments.translation: 但是我一直对任何与颜色有关的东西都感兴趣。

segments.text: Years ago, I went to a carpet shop with my parents when we were on holiday in Turkey, and I remember all the amazing colours.
segments.translation: 几年前，我和父母去土耳其度假时去了一家地毯店，我记得那些所有令人惊艳的颜色。

segments.text: TUTOR: They might not all have been natural dyes.
segments.translation: 导师：他们可能并不都是天然染料。

segments.text: JIM: Maybe not, but for the project I decided to follow it up.
segments.translation: 吉姆：也许不是，但为了项目，我决定继续研究这个话题。

segments.text: And I found a great book about a botanic garden in California that specialises in plants used for dyes.
segments.translation: 我还发现了一本很棒的书，是关于加利福尼亚的一个专门种植用于染料的植物园。

segments.text: TUTOR: OK. So, in your project, you had to include a practical investigation.
segments.translation: 导师：好的。所以，在你的项目中，你必须有一个实际的调查。

segments.text: JIM: Yeah. At first I couldn't decide on my variables.
segments.translation: 吉姆：是的。一开始我无法决定变量。

segments.text: I was going to just look at one type of fibre for example, like cotton ...
segments.translation: 我只看一种纤维，例如棉花……

segments.text: TUTOR: ... and see how different types of dyes affected it?
segments.translation: 导师：还要观察不同类型的染料如何影响它?

segments.text: JIM: Yes. Then I decided to include others as well, so I looked at cotton and wool and nylon.
segments.translation: 吉姆：是的。然后我决定把其他的也包括进来，所以我观察了棉、羊毛和尼龙。

segments.text: TUTOR: With just one type of dye?
segments.translation: 导师：只用一种染料?

segments.text: JIM: Various types, including some that weren't natural, for comparison.
segments.translation: 吉姆：各种类型的。包括一些不是纯天然的染料来做比较。

segments.text: TUTOR: OK.
segments.translation: 导师：好的。

segments.text: JIM: So, I did the experiments last week.
segments.translation: 吉姆：所以，我上周做了实验。

segments.text: I used some ready-made natural dyes, I found a website which supplied them, they came in just a few days, but I also made some of my own.
segments.translation: 我用了一些现成的天然染料，我找到了一个销售网站，几天就收到了，但我也自己做了一些。

segments.text: TUTOR: That must have taken quite a bit of time.
segments.translation: 导师：这一定会花相当多的时间。

segments.text: JIM: Yes, I'd thought it'd just be a matter of a teaspoon or so of dye, and actually that wasn't the case at all.
segments.translation: 吉姆：是的，我认为只需要用大约一茶匙的染料，实际上并非如此。

segments.text: Like I was using one vegetable, beetroot, for a red dye, and I had to chop up a whole pile of it.
segments.translation: 我用了一种蔬菜，甜菜根，做红色的染料。我不得不把它整个切碎。

segments.text: So it all took longer than I'd expected.
segments.translation: 所以花的时间比我预想的要长。

segments.text: TUTOR: One possibility is to use food colourings.
segments.translation: 导师：有一种可能的方法是使用食品色素。

segments.text: JIM: I did use one. That was a yellow dye, an artificial one.
segments.translation: 吉姆：我用过一个。那是一种黄色染料，一种人造染料。

segments.text: TUTOR: Tartrazine?
segments.translation: 导师：柠檬黄?

segments.text: JIM: Yeah. I used it on cotton first.
segments.translation: 吉姆：是的。我先用在棉花上。

segments.text: It came out a great colour, but when I rinsed the material, the colour just washed away.
segments.translation: 它呈现的颜色很好，但当我冲洗材料时，颜色就被洗掉了。

segments.text: I'd been going to try it out on nylon, but I abandoned that idea.
segments.translation: 我本来打算在尼龙上试试，但我放弃了这个想法。

segments.text: TUTOR: Were you worried about health issues?
segments.translation: 导师：你担心健康问题吗?

segments.text: JIM: I'd thought if it's a legal food colouring, it must be safe.
segments.translation: 吉姆：我想如果这是一个合法的食用色素，它必须是安全的。

segments.text: TUTOR: Well, it can occasionally cause allergic reactions, I believe.
segments.translation: 老师：嗯， 我相信它有时也会引起过敏反应。

segments.text: TUTOR: So what natural dyes did you look at?
segments.translation: 导师：那么你在考虑哪些天然染料?

segments.text: JIM: Well, one was turmeric.
segments.translation: 吉姆：嗯，一个是姜黄，

segments.text: The colour's great, it's a really strong yellow.
segments.translation: 颜色很好，是一种很深的黄色。

segments.text: It's generally used in dishes like curry.
segments.translation: 它通常用于像咖喱这样的菜肴中。

segments.text: TUTOR: It's meant to be quite good for your health when eaten, but you might find it's not permanent when it's used as a dye - a few washes, and it's gone.
segments.translation: 导师：食用时，它似乎对你的健康很好，但你可能会发现用作染料时，它不是永久性的，经过几次冲洗，它就掉色了。

segments.text: JIM: Right. I used beetroot as a dye for wool.
segments.translation: 吉姆：是的。我用甜菜根作羊毛的染料。

segments.text: When I chop up beetroot to eat I always end up with bright red hands, but the wool ended up just a sort of watery cream shade. Disappointing.
segments.translation: 当我切甜菜根吃的时候，我的手总是红彤彤的，但是羊毛最后变成了一种稀奶油色。令人失望。

segments.text: TUTOR: There's a natural dye called Tyrian purple.
segments.translation: 导师：有一种叫做泰尔红紫的天然染料。

segments.text: Have you heard of that?
segments.translation: 你听说过吗?

segments.text: JIM: Yes. It comes from a shellfish, and it was worn in ancient times but only by important people as it was so rare.
segments.translation: 吉姆：听说过。它来自一种贝类，在古代因为它非常罕见，只有重要人物才能穿这种颜色。

segments.text: I didn't use it.
segments.translation: 我没有用过它。

segments.text: TUTOR: It fell out of use centuries ago, though one researcher managed to get hold of some recently.
segments.translation: 导师：几个世纪前它不用了，尽管最近一位研究人员设法找到了一些。

segments.text: But that shade of purple can be produced by chemical dyes nowadays.
segments.translation: 但是现在那种紫色的色度可以用化学染料染出来。

segments.text: Did you use any black dyes?
segments.translation: 你用过黑色染料吗?

segments.text: JIM: Logwood. That was quite complicated.
segments.translation: 吉姆：洋苏木。这种染料使用相当复杂。

segments.text: I had to prepare the fabric so the dye would take.
segments.translation: 我需要提前处理好布料，这样染料才能上色。

segments.text: TUTOR: I hope you were careful to wear gloves.
segments.translation: 导师：我希望你小心，要戴手套。

segments.text: JIM: Yes. I know the danger with that dye.
segments.translation: 吉姆：好的。我知道那种染料有危险。

segments.text: TUTOR: Good. It can be extremely dangerous if it's ingested.
segments.translation: 导师：很好。如果你摄入它，会非常危险。

segments.text: Now, presumably you had a look at an insect-based dye?
segments.translation: 现在，也许你考虑过一种昆虫染料?

segments.text: Like cochineal, for example?
segments.translation: 比如说胭脂红?

segments.text: JIM: Yes. I didn't actually make that, I didn't have time to start crushing up insects to get the red colour and anyway they're not available here, but I managed to get the dye quite easily from a website.
segments.translation: 吉姆：是的。我并没有做那个，我没有时间去碾碎昆虫来得到那种红色，这不容易实现，但是我很容易地从一个网站上得到了染料。

segments.text: But it cost a fortune.
segments.translation: 但它花了我一大笔钱。

segments.text: I can see why it's generally just used in cooking, and in small quantities.
segments.translation: 我可以理解为什么人们只在烹饪中少量地使用这种染料了。

segments.text: TUTOR: Yes, it's very effective, but that's precisely why it's not used as a dye.
segments.translation: 导师：是的，它非常有效的，但这正是为什么不被用作染料的原因。

segments.text: JIM: I also read about using metal oxide.
segments.translation: 吉姆：我也读过关于使用金属氧化物的相关读物。

segments.text: Apparently you can allow iron to rust while it's in contact with the fabric, and that colours it.
segments.translation: 很明显，当铁接触织物时，会让它生锈，从而使织物变色。

segments.text: TUTOR: Yes, that works well for dying cotton.
segments.translation: 导师：是的，它适用于染棉质织物。

segments.text: But you have to be careful as the metal can actually affect the fabric and so you can't expect to get a lot of wear out of fabrics treated in this way.
segments.translation: 但是你必须小心，因为金属会影响织物，所以不要期待通过这种方法染出的织物的还能很耐用。

segments.text: And the colours are quite subtle, not everyone likes them.
segments.translation: 并且织物的颜色很微妙，不是每个人都喜欢。

segments.text: Anyway, it looks as if you've done a lot of work ...
segments.translation: 总之，看起来你做了很多工作……
